zh.lifeSummary: 當Friedrich I. Herzog von Schwaben於大約1047年出生在德國爾特波格格皮根霍恩斯陶分時，他的父親Friedrich von Büren29歲，母親Hildegard von Egisheim-Dagsburg32歲。他於1086年與Agnes von Waiblingen Herzogin von Schwaben und Markgräfin Marcha orientalis結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1105年4月6日在德國巴登-爾特波格藍德可萊斯 格皮根享年59歲，埋葬在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇艾爾茲比斯圖姆 麥音洛其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Friedrich I. Herzog von Schwaben于大约1047年出生在德国爾特波格格皮根霍恩斯陶分时，他的父亲Friedrich von Büren29岁，母亲Hildegard von Egisheim-Dagsburg32岁。他于1086年与Agnes von Waiblingen Herzogin von Schwaben und Markgräfin Marcha orientalis结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1105年4月6日在德国巴登-爾特波格藍德可萊斯 格皮根享年59岁，埋葬在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇艾爾茲比斯圖姆 麥音洛其。

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Schwaben
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German (Schäben): unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: Friedrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power’; a royal and imperial name from an early date.

variants.name: Frederick
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Friederich
meanings: from frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power’; a royal and imperial name from an early date.

en.lifeSummary: When Friedrich I. Herzog von Schwaben was born about 1047, in Hohenstaufen, Göppingen, Württemberg, Germany, his father, Friedrich von Büren, was 29 and his mother, Hildegard von Egisheim-Dagsburg, was 32. He married Agnes von Waiblingen Herzogin von Schwaben und Markgräfin Marcha orientalis in 1086. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 6 April 1105, in Göppingen, Baden-Württemberg, Germany, at the age of 59, and was buried in Lorch, Erzbistum Mainz, Holy Roman Empire.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Friedrich I. Herzog von Schwaben est né environ 1047, à Hohenstaufen, Göppingen, Württemberg, Allemagne, son père, Friedrich von Büren, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Hildegard von Egisheim-Dagsburg, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Agnes von Waiblingen Herzogin von Schwaben und Markgräfin Marcha orientalis en 1086. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 6 avril 1105, à Landkreis Göppingen, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Lorch, Erzbistum Mainz, Saint-Empire romain.

de.lifeSummary: Als Friedrich I. Herzog von Schwaben um 1047 in Hohenstaufen, Göppingen, Württemberg, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Friedrich von Büren 29 und seine Mutter Hildegard von Egisheim-Dagsburg 32. Er hat 1086 Agnes von Waiblingen Herzogin von Schwaben und Markgräfin Marcha orientalis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 6. April 1105 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Landkreis Göppingen, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland und wurde in Lorch, Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Friedrich I. Herzog von Schwaben è nato il circa 1047 nel Hohenstaufen, Göppingen, Württemberg, Germania, il suo padre, Friedrich von Büren, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Hildegard von Egisheim-Dagsburg, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Agnes von Waiblingen Herzogin von Schwaben und Markgräfin Marcha orientalis nel 1086. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 6 aprile 1105, nel Landkreis Göppingen, Baden-Württemberg, Germania, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Lorch, Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich.

ko.lifeSummary: Friedrich I. Herzog von Schwaben(이)가 대략 1047년 독일 wu르테버그 그o핀겐 호헨스타펜에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Friedrich von Büren(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Hildegard von Egisheim-Dagsburg(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1086년 Agnes von Waiblingen Herzogin von Schwaben und Markgräfin Marcha orientalis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1105년 4월 6일 독일 바데-wu르테버그 랜드크레이스 그o핀겐에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 르즈비스투 맹츠 로르치에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Friedrich I. Herzog von Schwaben родился около 1047 г. в местности хохенстауфен, гoппинген, вuрттембирг, Германия, его отцу по имени Friedrich von Büren было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Hildegard von Egisheim-Dagsburg, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes von Waiblingen Herzogin von Schwaben und Markgräfin Marcha orientalis в 1086 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 6 апреля 1105 г. в местности ландкрис гoппинген, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности лорх, эрзбистум меинз, хилигесь рoмищесь раич.

Se casó con Agnes von Waiblingen Herzogin von Schwaben und Markgräfin Marcha orientalis en 1086. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 6 de abril de 1105, en Landkreis Göppingen, Baden-Württemberg, Alemania, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Lorch, Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich.
